old_claim.note: Clinical Observation & Diagnosis provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because field wound care does not require the later source-backed clinical-diagnosis node.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica Diagnosis overview describes diagnosis generally; it does not establish formal clinical diagnosis as a prerequisite for battlefield wound care.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The cited diagnosis source supports a broad medical concept, not the specific edge from battlefield medicine to formal clinical observation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Military field medicine can arise from wound treatment, surgery, herbal remedies, and organized armies; requiring later formal clinical diagnosis overstates the edge.

ontology_before: Military field medicine treated Clinical Observation & Diagnosis as an enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: Military field medicine keeps surgical, herbal, and organized-military prerequisites without depending on later formal diagnostic practice.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Military field medicine must not directly depend on Clinical Observation & Diagnosis unless the node is narrowed to formal diagnostic military medicine.
would_reject_if: A source shows this node is scoped only to formal diagnostic military medicine.
The clinical observation node is redated earlier and sourced as a direct prerequisite for battlefield treatment.
A source demonstrates battlefield medicine did not exist independently of formal diagnostic systems.
short_reason: Battlefield wound care does not require later formal clinical diagnosis as a prerequisite.
